text: Rongfang Shen

title: Generate Reference Gene Matrix from RNA-seq DEGs — generateRef_rnaseq
code: dds <- matrix(rpois(200 * 10, lambda = 10), ncol = 10) rownames(dds) <- paste("Gene", 1:200, sep = "_") colnames(dds) <- paste("Sample", 1:10, sep = "_") pheno <- sample(c("Type1", "Type2", "Type3"), 10, replace = TRUE) dat <- matrix(rnorm(200 * 10), ncol = 10) rownames(dat) <- rownames(dds) colnames(dat) <- colnames(dds) # \donttest{ results <- generateRef_rnaseq(dds = dds, pheno = pheno, FDR = 0.05, dat = dat) #> converting counts to integer mode #> Warning: some variables in design formula are characters, converting to factors #> estimating size factors #> estimating dispersions #> gene-wise dispersion estimates #> mean-dispersion relationship #> final dispersion estimates #> fitting model and testing #> converting counts to integer mode #> Warning: some variables in design formula are characters, converting to factors #> estimating size factors #> estimating dispersions #> gene-wise dispersion estimates #> mean-dispersion relationship #> -- note: fitType='parametric', but the dispersion trend was not well captured by the #>    function: y = a/x + b, and a local regression fit was automatically substituted. #>    specify fitType='local' or 'mean' to avoid this message next time. #> final dispersion estimates #> fitting model and testing #> converting counts to integer mode #> Warning: some variables in design formula are characters, converting to factors #> estimating size factors #> estimating dispersions #> gene-wise dispersion estimates #> mean-dispersion relationship #> -- note: fitType='parametric', but the dispersion trend was not well captured by the #>    function: y = a/x + b, and a local regression fit was automatically substituted. #>    specify fitType='local' or 'mean' to avoid this message next time. #> final dispersion estimates #> fitting model and testing # }

what: Generate Reference Matrix from Seurat Object — generateRef_seurat
title: Generate Reference Matrix from Seurat Object — generateRef_seurat
text: Generates reference gene expression data Seurat object identifying marker genes cell type aggregating expression data.

title: Generate Reference Matrix from Seurat Object — generateRef_seurat
code: generateRef_seurat(   sce,   celltype = NULL,   proportion = NULL,   assay_deg = "RNA",   slot_deg = "data",   adjust_assay = FALSE,   assay_out = "RNA",   slot_out = "data",   verbose = FALSE,   only.pos = TRUE,   n_ref_genes = 50,   logfc.threshold = 0.15,   test.use = "wilcox" )

title: Generate Reference Matrix from Seurat Object — generateRef_seurat
text: sce Seurat object containing single-cell RNA-seq data. celltype Character. Cell type column name metadata. Default `NULL` (uses default identity). proportion Numeric. Proportion cells randomly select analysis. Default `NULL` (use cells). assay_deg Character. Assay finding markers. Default `"RNA"`. slot_deg Character. Slot finding markers. Default `"data"`. adjust_assay Logical. Whether adjust assay SCT. Default `FALSE`. assay_out Character. Assay output. Default `"RNA"`. slot_out Character. Slot output. Default `"data"`. verbose Logical. Print verbose messages. Default `FALSE`. .pos Logical. Return positive markers. Default `TRUE`. n_ref_genes Integer. Number reference genes per cell type. Default 50. logfc.threshold Numeric. Log fold change threshold. Default 0.15. test.use Character. Statistical test marker identification. Default `"wilcox"`.

title: Generate Reference Matrix from Seurat Object — generateRef_seurat
text: Matrix containing aggregated expression data reference genes.

title: Generate Reference Matrix from Seurat Object — generateRef_seurat
text: Dongqiang Zeng

title: Generate Reference Matrix from Seurat Object — generateRef_seurat
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ if (requireNamespace("Seurat", quietly = TRUE)) {   # Requires a Seurat object with sufficient cells and markers   sm <- generateRef_seurat(sce = seurat_obj, celltype = "cell_type", slot_out = "data") } } # }

text: Dorothee Nickles Dongqiang Zeng

title: Extract Hazard Ratio and Confidence Intervals from Cox Model — getHRandCIfromCoxph
code: library(survival) set.seed(123) df <- data.frame(   TTE = rexp(200, rate = 0.1),   Cens = rbinom(200, size = 1, prob = 0.7),   group = sample(c("Treatment", "Control"), 200, replace = TRUE) ) coxphData <- survival::coxph(survival::Surv(TTE, Cens) ~ group, data = df) results <- getHRandCIfromCoxph(coxphData) print(results) #>                       P    HR CI_low_0.95 CI_up_0.95 #> groupTreatment 0.400853 0.868      0.6238     1.2077

what: Set and View Color Palettes — get_cols
title: Set and View Color Palettes — get_cols
text: Retrieves color palettes IOBR package options randomization visualization. Users can specify predefined palettes provide custom colors.

title: Set and View Color Palettes — get_cols
code: get_cols(cols = "normal", palette = 1, show_col = TRUE, seed = 123)

title: Set and View Color Palettes — get_cols
text: cols Character vector colors, one : - `"normal"`: Use standard palette - `"random"`: Randomly shuffle palette Default `"normal"`. palette Numeric character specifying palette. Options 1, 2, 3, 4, palette name. Default 1. show_col Logical indicating whether display color palette. Default `TRUE`. seed Integer seed random number generator `cols = "random"`. Default 123.

title: Set and View Color Palettes — get_cols
text: Character vector colors.

title: Set and View Color Palettes — get_cols
code: # Get default palette mycols <- get_cols() #> '#E64B35FF', '#4DBBD5FF', '#00A087FF', '#3C5488FF', '#F39B7FFF', '#8491B4FF', '#91D1C2FF', '#DC0000FF', '#7E6148FF'   # Get random palette mycols <- get_cols(cols = "random", seed = 456) #> ℹ Random palettes: 1 (palette1), 2 (palette2), 3 (palette3), 4 (palette4) #> ℹ Using random seed: 456   # Use custom colors mycols <- get_cols(cols = c("red", "blue", "green"))

what: Calculate and Visualize Correlation Between Two Variables — get_cor
title: Calculate and Visualize Correlation Between Two Variables — get_cor
text: Calculates visualizes correlation two variables options scaling, handling missing values, incorporating grouping data.

title: Calculate and Visualize Correlation Between Two Variables — get_cor
code: get_cor(   eset,   pdata = NULL,   var1,   var2,   is.matrix = FALSE,   id_eset = "ID",   id_pdata = "ID",   scale = TRUE,   subtype = NULL,   na.subtype.rm = FALSE,   color_subtype = NULL,   palette = "jama",   index = NULL,   method = c("spearman", "pearson", "kendall"),   show_cor_result = TRUE,   col_line = NULL,   id = NULL,   show_label = FALSE,   point_size = 4,   title = NULL,   alpha = 0.5,   title_size = 1.5,   text_size = 10,   axis_angle = 0,   hjust = 0,   show_plot = TRUE,   save_plot = FALSE,   path = NULL,   fig.format = "png",   fig.width = 7,   fig.height = 7.3,   add.hdr.line = FALSE )

title: Calculate and Visualize Correlation Between Two Variables — get_cor
text: eset Dataset containing variables (data frame matrix). pdata Optional phenotype data frame. Default `NULL`. var1 Name first variable. var2 Name second variable. .matrix Logical indicating `eset` matrix features rows. Default `FALSE`. id_eset ID column `eset`. Default `"ID"`. id_pdata ID column `pdata`. Default `"ID"`. scale Logical indicating whether scale data. Default `TRUE`. subtype Optional grouping variable coloring points. Default `NULL`. na.subtype.rm Logical indicating whether remove NA subtype. Default `FALSE`. color_subtype Colors subtypes. Default `NULL`. palette Color palette name. Default `"jama"`. index Plot index filename. Default `NULL` (uses 1). method Correlation method: `"spearman"`, `"pearson"`, `"kendall"`. Default `"spearman"`. show_cor_result Logical indicating whether print correlation result. Default `TRUE`. col_line Color regression line. Default `NULL` (auto-determine). id Column point labels. Default `NULL`. show_label Logical indicating whether show labels. Default `FALSE`. point_size Size points. Default 4. title Plot title. Default `NULL`. alpha Transparency points. Default 0.5. title_size Title font size. Default 1.5. text_size Text font size. Default 10. axis_angle Axis label angle. Default 0. hjust Horizontal justification. Default 0. show_plot Logical indicating whether display plot. Default `TRUE`. save_plot Logical indicating whether save plot. Default `FALSE`. path Save path. Default `NULL`. fig.format Figure format: `"png"` `"pdf"`. Default `"png"`. fig.width Figure width inches. Default 7. fig.height Figure height inches. Default 7.3. add.hdr.line Logical adding HDR (high density region) lines. Default `FALSE`.

title: Calculate and Visualize Correlation Between Two Variables — get_cor
text: ggplot object correlation plot.

title: Calculate and Visualize Correlation Between Two Variables — get_cor
text: Dongqiang Zeng

title: Calculate and Visualize Correlation Between Two Variables — get_cor
code: eset_tme_stad <- load_data("eset_tme_stad") #> ℹ Loading cached data: "eset_tme_stad" get_cor(   eset = eset_tme_stad,   is.matrix = TRUE,   var1 = "GZMB",   var2 = "CD274" ) #> ℹ Calculating spearman correlation (n = 375) #>  #> 	Spearman's rank correlation rho #>  #> data:  data[[var1]] and data[[var2]] #> S = 2737736, p-value < 2.2e-16 #> alternative hypothesis: true rho is not equal to 0 #> sample estimates: #>       rho  #> 0.6885043  #>  #> ℹ Exact p-value: 5.4e-54 #> `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

what: Calculate and Visualize Correlation Matrix Between Two Variable Sets — get_cor_matrix
title: Calculate and Visualize Correlation Matrix Between Two Variable Sets — get_cor_matrix
text: Calculates visualizes correlation matrix two sets variables. Supports Pearson, Spearman, Kendall correlation methods. function generates customizable heatmap significance stars.

title: Calculate and Visualize Correlation Matrix Between Two Variable Sets — get_cor_matrix
code: get_cor_matrix(   data,   feas1,   feas2,   method = c("pearson", "spearman", "kendall"),   path = NULL,   index = 1,   fig.type = "pdf",   width = NULL,   height = NULL,   project = NULL,   is.matrix = FALSE,   scale = TRUE,   font.size = 15,   fill_by_cor = FALSE,   round.num = 1,   font.size.star = 8,   cols = NULL )

title: Calculate and Visualize Correlation Matrix Between Two Variable Sets — get_cor_matrix
text: data Input data frame matrix. Variables columns. feas1 Character vector variable names first set. feas2 Character vector variable names second set. method Correlation method: `"pearson"`, `"spearman"`, `"kendall"`. Default `"pearson"`. path Directory save plot. `NULL`, plot saved. Default `NULL`. index Numeric prefix output filename. Default 1. fig.type File format: `"pdf"`, `"png"`, etc. Default `"pdf"`. width Plot width inches. Auto-calculated `NULL`. height Plot height inches. Auto-calculated `NULL`. project Project name plot title. Default `NULL`. .matrix Logical: `TRUE`, data transposed. Default `FALSE`. scale Logical: scale variables correlation. Default `TRUE`. font.size Font size axis labels. Default 15. fill_by_cor Logical: show correlation values instead stars. Default `FALSE`. round.num Decimal places correlation values. Default 1. font.size.star Font size significance stars. Default 8. cols Custom colors gradient (low, mid, high). `NULL`, uses blue-white-red. Default `NULL`.

title: Calculate and Visualize Correlation Matrix Between Two Variable Sets — get_cor_matrix
text: ggplot object displaying correlation matrix heatmap.

title: Calculate and Visualize Correlation Matrix Between Two Variable Sets — get_cor_matrix
text: Dongqiang Zeng

title: Calculate and Visualize Correlation Matrix Between Two Variable Sets — get_cor_matrix
code: set.seed(123) data <- as.data.frame(matrix(rnorm(1000), nrow = 100, ncol = 10)) colnames(data) <- paste0("Gene_", 1:10)  feas1 <- c("Gene_1", "Gene_2", "Gene_3") feas2 <- c("Gene_4", "Gene_5", "Gene_6")  cor_plot <- get_cor_matrix(   data = data,   feas1 = feas1,   feas2 = feas2,   method = "spearman",   project = "Example Correlation" ) #> ℹ Calculating spearman correlation: 3 x 3

title: Get Default Download Mirrors — get_default_mirrors
text: Returns default list download mirrors.

title: Get Default Download Mirrors — get_default_mirrors
code: get_default_mirrors()

title: Get Default Download Mirrors — get_default_mirrors
text: Character vector mirror URLs.

title: Get IOBR Cache Directory — get_iobr_cache_dir
text: Returns current cache directory IOBR downloaded data. cache directory determined following priority order: 1. Function argument `cache_dir` (provided) 2. Option `IOBR.cache_dir` (set via `options()`) 3. Default system cache location via `tools::R_user_dir()`

title: Get IOBR Cache Directory — get_iobr_cache_dir
code: get_iobr_cache_dir(cache_dir = NULL)

title: Get IOBR Cache Directory — get_iobr_cache_dir
text: cache_dir Optional character string override current setting.

title: Get IOBR Cache Directory — get_iobr_cache_dir
text: Character string cache directory path.

title: Get IOBR Cache Directory — get_iobr_cache_dir
code: # Get current cache directory get_iobr_cache_dir() #> [1] "/tmp/Rtmpl7xRpT"  # Set custom cache directory via options (use tempdir() for examples) options(IOBR.cache_dir = tempdir()) get_iobr_cache_dir() #> [1] "/tmp/Rtmpl7xRpT"

what: Extract Top Marker Genes from Single-Cell Differential Results — get_sig_sc
title: Extract Top Marker Genes from Single-Cell Differential Results — get_sig_sc
text: Selects top N marker genes per cluster ranked differential expression result table.

title: Extract Top Marker Genes from Single-Cell Differential Results — get_sig_sc
code: get_sig_sc(   deg,   cluster = "cluster",   gene = "gene",   avg_log2FC = "avg_log2FC",   n = 100 )

title: Extract Top Marker Genes from Single-Cell Differential Results — get_sig_sc
text: deg Data frame matrix. Ranked marker statistics. cluster Character. Column name containing cluster identifiers. Default `"cluster"`. gene Character. Column name containing gene identifiers. Default `"gene"`. avg_log2FC Character. Column name average log2 fold change. Default `"avg_log2FC"`. n Integer. Number top markers per cluster. Default 100.

title: Extract Top Marker Genes from Single-Cell Differential Results — get_sig_sc
text: List character vectors; element contains top N genes   cluster.

title: GSVA API Version Detection — gsva_use_new_api
text: Detects whether installed GSVA package supports new parameter-based API (gsvaParam/ssgseaParam) old direct argument API. function used internally ensure compatibility across different GSVA package versions (1.50.0+ vs older versions).

title: GSVA API Version Detection — gsva_use_new_api
code: gsva_use_new_api()

title: GSVA API Version Detection — gsva_use_new_api
text: list two elements:   - `use_new_api`: Logical indicating whether use new API (`TRUE`)     old API (`FALSE`)   - `gsva_version`: Character string installed GSVA version,     `"installed"` available

what: Identify High-Variance Features from Statistical Results — high_var_fea
title: Identify High-Variance Features from Statistical Results — high_var_fea
text: Selects top variable (- -regulated) features based adjusted p-value log fold-change thresholds.

title: Identify High-Variance Features from Statistical Results — high_var_fea
code: high_var_fea(   result,   target,   name_padj = "padj",   padj_cutoff = 1,   name_logfc,   logfc_cutoff = 0,   n = 10,   data_type = NULL )

title: Identify High-Variance Features from Statistical Results — high_var_fea
text: result Data frame tibble. Statistical results containing feature, adjusted p-value, logFC columns. target Character. Column name feature identifiers. name_padj Character. Adjusted p-value column name. Default `"padj"`. padj_cutoff Numeric. Adjusted p-value threshold. Default 1. name_logfc Character. log2 fold-change column name. logfc_cutoff Numeric. Absolute log2 fold-change threshold. Default 0. n Integer. Number top top features select. Default 10. data_type Character `NULL`. `"survival"`, adjusts logFC interpretation. Default `NULL`.

title: Identify High-Variance Features from Statistical Results — high_var_fea
text: Character vector selected feature names (combined sets).

title: Identify High-Variance Features from Statistical Results — high_var_fea
text: Dongqiang Zeng

title: Identify High-Variance Features from Statistical Results — high_var_fea
code: result_data <- data.frame(   gene = c("Gene1", "Gene2", "Gene3", "Gene4", "Gene5"),   padj = c(0.01, 0.02, 0.05, 0.001, 0.03),   logfc = c(-2, 1.5, -3, 2.5, 0.5) ) high_var_fea(   result = result_data,   target = "gene",   name_padj = "padj",   name_logfc = "logfc",   n = 2,   padj_cutoff = 0.05,   logfc_cutoff = 1.5 ) #> ! Cutoff too strict for down-regulated features, only 1 found #> ! Cutoff too strict for up-regulated features, only 1 found #> [1] "Gene1" "Gene4"

what: IMvigor210 Bladder Cancer Immunotherapy Cohort Data — imvigor210_pdata
title: IMvigor210 Bladder Cancer Immunotherapy Cohort Data — imvigor210_pdata
text: Clinical biomarker data IMvigor210 clinical trial cohort. Includes treatment response, survival outcomes, immune biomarker measurements bladder cancer patients treated atezolizumab.

title: IMvigor210 Bladder Cancer Immunotherapy Cohort Data — imvigor210_pdata
code: data(imvigor210_pdata)

title: IMvigor210 Bladder Cancer Immunotherapy Cohort Data — imvigor210_pdata
text: data frame patients rows variables columns: ID Patient sample identifier BOR Best overall response (CR, PR, SD, PD, NA) BOR_binary Binary response classification (R=responder,   NR=non-responder) OS_days Overall survival time days OS_status Overall survival status (0=alive, 1=dead) Mutation_Load Tumor mutation burden Neo_antigen_Load Neoantigen load CD_8_T_effector CD8+ T effector signature score Immune_Checkpoint Immune checkpoint signature score Pan_F_TBRs Pan-fibroblast TGF-\(\beta\) response signature Mismatch_Repair Mismatch repair status signature TumorPurity Estimated tumor purity

what: Integrative Correlation Analysis Between Phenotype and Features — iobr_cor_plot
title: Integrative Correlation Analysis Between Phenotype and Features — iobr_cor_plot
text: Performs comprehensive correlation analysis phenotype data feature data, supporting continuous categorical phenotypes. Filters features based statistical significance generates publication-ready visualizations including box plots, heatmaps, correlation plots.

title: Integrative Correlation Analysis Between Phenotype and Features — iobr_cor_plot
code: iobr_cor_plot(   pdata_group,   id1 = "ID",   feature_data,   id2 = "ID",   target = NULL,   group = "group3",   is_target_continuous = TRUE,   padj_cutoff = 1,   index = 1,   category = "signature",   signature_group = NULL,   ProjectID = "TCGA",   palette_box = "nrc",   cols_box = NULL,   palette_corplot = "pheatmap",   palette_heatmap = 2,   feature_limit = 26,   character_limit = 60,   show_heatmap_col_name = FALSE,   show_col = FALSE,   show_plot = FALSE,   path = NULL,   discrete_x = 20,   discrete_width = 20,   show_palettes = FALSE,   fig.type = "pdf" )

title: Integrative Correlation Analysis Between Phenotype and Features — iobr_cor_plot
text: pdata_group Data frame containing phenotype data identifier column. id1 Character string specifying column name `pdata_group` serving sample identifier. Default `"ID"`. feature_data Data frame containing feature data corresponding identifiers. id2 Character string specifying column name `feature_data` serving sample identifier. Default `"ID"`. target Character string specifying target variable column name continuous analysis. Default `NULL`. group Character string specifying grouping variable name categorical analysis. Default `"group3"`. is_target_continuous Logical indicating whether target variable continuous, affects grouping strategy. Default `TRUE`. padj_cutoff Numeric value specifying adjusted p-value cutoff filtering features. Default `1`. index Numeric index used ordering output file names. Default `1`. category Character string specifying data category: `"signature"` `"gene"`. signature_group List specifying grouping variable signatures. Options include `"sig_group"` signature grouping `"signature_collection"`/`"signature_tme"` gene grouping. ProjectID Character string specifying project identifier file naming. palette_box Character string integer specifying color palette box plots. Default `"nrc"`. cols_box Character vector specific colors box plots. Default `NULL`. palette_corplot Character string integer specifying color palette correlation plots. Default `"pheatmap"`. palette_heatmap Integer specifying color palette index heatmaps. Default `2`. feature_limit Integer specifying maximum number features display. Default `26`. character_limit Integer specifying maximum number characters variable labels. Default `60`. show_heatmap_col_name Logical indicating whether display column names heatmaps. Default `FALSE`. show_col Logical indicating whether display color codes palettes. Default `FALSE`. show_plot Logical indicating whether display plots. Default `FALSE`. path Character string specifying directory path saving output files. Default `NULL`. discrete_x Numeric threshold character length beyond labels discretized. Default `20`. discrete_width Numeric value specifying width label wrapping plots. Default `20`. show_palettes Logical indicating whether display color palettes. Default `FALSE`. fig.type Character string specifying format saving figures (`"pdf"`, `"png"`, etc.). Default `"pdf"`.

title: Integrative Correlation Analysis Between Phenotype and Features — iobr_cor_plot
text: Depending configuration, returns ggplot2 objects (box plots, heatmaps,   correlation plots) /data frame containing statistical analysis results.

title: Integrative Correlation Analysis Between Phenotype and Features — iobr_cor_plot
text: Dongqiang Zeng

title: Integrative Correlation Analysis Between Phenotype and Features — iobr_cor_plot
code: set.seed(123)  pdata_group <- data.frame(   ID = 1:100,   phenotype_score = rnorm(100) )  feature_data <- data.frame(   ID = 1:100,   Feature1 = rnorm(100),   Feature2 = rnorm(100),   Feature3 = rnorm(100) )  sig_group_example <- list(   signature = c("Feature1", "Feature2", "Feature3") )  results <- iobr_cor_plot(   pdata_group = pdata_group,   feature_data = feature_data,   id1 = "ID",   id2 = "ID",   target = "phenotype_score",   is_target_continuous = TRUE,   category = "signature",   signature_group = sig_group_example,   show_plot = FALSE,   path = tempdir() ) #> ℹ Processing signature: "signature" #> tidyHeatmap says: (once per session) from release 1.7.0 the scaling is set to "none" by default. Please use scale = "row", "column" or "both" to apply scaling #> tidyHeatmap says: If you use tidyHeatmap for scientific research, please cite: Mangiola, S. and Papenfuss, A.T., 2020. 'tidyHeatmap: an R package for modular heatmap production based on tidy principles.' Journal of Open Source Software. doi:10.21105/joss.02472. #> This message is displayed once per session. #> Warning: `when()` was deprecated in purrr 1.0.0. #> ℹ Please use `if` instead. #> ℹ The deprecated feature was likely used in the tidyHeatmap package. #>   Please report the issue at #>   <https://github.com/stemangiola/tidyHeatmap/issues>. #> ℹ Heatmap3 palettes: pheatmap, peach, blues, virids, reds, normal  #> ℹ Computing spearman correlation for 3 features #> ✔ Correlation analysis complete  print(results) #> # A tibble: 3 × 6 #>   sig_names p.value statistic p.adj log10pvalue stars #>   <chr>       <dbl>     <dbl> <dbl>       <dbl> <fct> #> 1 Feature2    0.225  -0.122   0.493      0.647  "+"   #> 2 Feature3    0.328  -0.0987  0.493      0.484  "+"   #> 3 Feature1    0.969   0.00390 0.969      0.0136 ""

title: Tumor Microenvironment (TME) Deconvolution Pipeline — iobr_deconvo_pipeline
text: Executes integrated TME analysis gene expression matrix: performs immune/stromal cell deconvolution using multiple algorithms, computes signature scores, aggregates results. Designed exploratory immunogenomic profiling.

title: Tumor Microenvironment (TME) Deconvolution Pipeline — iobr_deconvo_pipeline
code: iobr_deconvo_pipeline(   eset,   project,   array,   tumor_type,   path = NULL,   permutation = 1000 )

title: Tumor Microenvironment (TME) Deconvolution Pipeline — iobr_deconvo_pipeline
text: eset Numeric matrix. Gene expression (TPM/log scale) genes rows. project Character. Project name (used output naming). array Logical. Whether data originated array platform. Affects deconvolution choices. tumor_type Character. Tumor type code (e.g., "stad") used certain methods. path Character. Output directory. Default NULL (uses tempdir()). permutation Integer. Number permutations CIBERSORT (similar). Default 1000.

title: Tumor Microenvironment (TME) Deconvolution Pipeline — iobr_deconvo_pipeline
text: Data frame integrating cell fractions signature scores (also writes intermediate outputs disk).

title: Tumor Microenvironment (TME) Deconvolution Pipeline — iobr_deconvo_pipeline
text: Dongqiang Zeng

title: Tumor Microenvironment (TME) Deconvolution Pipeline — iobr_deconvo_pipeline

title: Differential Expression Analysis — iobr_deg
text: Performs differential expression analysis gene expression data using either DESeq2 limma. Includes pre-processing steps like filtering low count data, calculates fold changes adjusted p-values. Optionally generates volcano plots heatmaps.

title: Differential Expression Analysis — iobr_deg
code: iobr_deg(   eset,   annotation = NULL,   id_anno = NULL,   pdata,   group_id = "group",   pdata_id = "ID",   array = FALSE,   method = c("DESeq2", "limma"),   contrast = c("High", "Low"),   path = NULL,   padj_cutoff = 0.01,   logfc_cutoff = 0.5,   volcano_plot = FALSE,   col_volcano = 1,   heatmap = TRUE,   col_heatmap = 1,   parallel = FALSE )

title: Differential Expression Analysis — iobr_deg
text: eset matrix gene expression data rows represent genes columns represent samples. annotation Optional data frame mapping gene IDs gene names. Default `NULL`. id_anno Character string specifying identifier column annotation. Default `NULL`. pdata data frame containing sample information grouping labels. group_id Character string specifying column name `pdata` containing grouping labels. Default `"group"`. pdata_id Character string specifying column name `pdata` sample IDs. Default `"ID"`. array Logical indicating whether perform quantile normalization. Default `FALSE`. method Character string specifying method: `"DESeq2"` `"limma"`. Default `"DESeq2"`. contrast Character vector length 2 specifying contrast groups. Default `c("High", "Low")`. path Character string output directory. Default `NULL`. padj_cutoff Numeric cutoff adjusted p-values. Default `0.01`. logfc_cutoff Numeric log2 fold change cutoff. Default `0.5`. volcano_plot Logical indicating whether generate volcano plot. Default `FALSE`. col_volcano Integer specifying color index volcano plot. Default `1`. heatmap Logical indicating whether generate heatmap. Default `TRUE`. col_heatmap Integer specifying color index heatmap. Default `1`. parallel Logical indicating whether run parallel. Default `FALSE`.

title: Differential Expression Analysis — iobr_deg
text: Data frame containing differentially expressed genes statistics   including log2 fold changes adjusted p-values.

title: Differential Expression Analysis — iobr_deg
text: Dongqiang Zeng

title: Differential Expression Analysis — iobr_deg
code: # \donttest{ eset_stad <- load_data("eset_stad") #> ℹ Loading cached data: "eset_stad" stad_group <- load_data("stad_group") deg <- iobr_deg(   eset = eset_stad, pdata = stad_group,   group_id = "subtype", pdata_id = "ID", array = FALSE,   method = "DESeq2", contrast = c("EBV", "GS"),   path = file.path(tempdir(), "STAD") ) #> ℹ Matching grouping information and expression matrix #> ℹ Using DESeq2 for RNA-seq differential analysis #> ! Ensure `eset` is a count expression matrix #> converting counts to integer mode #> Warning: some variables in design formula are characters, converting to factors #> estimating size factors #> estimating dispersions #> gene-wise dispersion estimates #> mean-dispersion relationship #> final dispersion estimates #> fitting model and testing #> ℹ Differential analysis summary: #> ℹ   Adj.p < 0.001: 2509 #> ℹ   Adj.p < 0.05: 7446 #> ℹ   Adj.p < 0.1: 9565 #> ℹ   Adj.p < 0.25: 13933 #> ℹ Using built-in anno_grch38 for annotation #> ℹ Loading cached data: "anno_grch38" #> ℹ Group 1 = EBV: 5 samples #> ℹ Group 2 = GS: 5 samples #> ℹ Group 1 samples: TCGA-BR-6455, TCGA-BR-7196, TCGA-BR-8686, TCGA-BR-A4J4, TCGA-FP-7916 #> ℹ Group 2 samples: TCGA-BR-8371, TCGA-BR-8380, TCGA-BR-8592, TCGA-BR-A4IV, TCGA-BR-A4J9 #> ✔ DEG results written to: /tmp/Rtmpl7xRpT/STAD/2-DEGs.csv head(deg) #> # A tibble: 6 × 21 #>   row     baseMean log2FoldChange lfcSE  stat   pvalue     padj eff_length    gc #>   <chr>      <dbl>          <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl>      <dbl> <dbl> #> 1 ENSG00…    3256.           3.94 0.303  13.0 1.41e-38 4.93e-34       3156 0.519 #> 2 ENSG00…     312.           5.88 0.492  12.0 5.79e-33 1.01e-28       2494 0.510 #> 3 ENSG00…  128738.          -4.27 0.373 -11.5 2.15e-30 2.50e-26       9251 0.623 #> 4 ENSG00…  392400.          -4.95 0.436 -11.3 8.41e-30 7.33e-26       3811 0.498 #> 5 ENSG00…    3248.           2.55 0.231  11.1 1.78e-28 1.24e-24       9980 0.430 #> 6 ENSG00…    3225.           3.12 0.302  10.3 4.60e-25 2.67e-21       3545 0.521 #> # ℹ 12 more variables: entrez <dbl>, symbol <chr>, chr <chr>, start <dbl>, #> #   end <dbl>, strand <dbl>, biotype <chr>, description <chr>, sigORnot <chr>, #> #   label <chr>, EBV <dbl>, GS <dbl> # }

title: Principal Component Analysis (PCA) Visualization — iobr_pca
text: function performs Principal Component Analysis (PCA) gene expression data, reduces dimensionality preserving variance, generates scatter plot visualization.

title: Principal Component Analysis (PCA) Visualization — iobr_pca
code: iobr_pca(   data,   is.matrix = TRUE,   scale = TRUE,   is.log = FALSE,   pdata,   id_pdata = "ID",   group = NULL,   geom.ind = "point",   cols = "normal",   palette = "jama",   repel = FALSE,   ncp = 5,   axes = c(1, 2),   addEllipses = TRUE )

title: Principal Component Analysis (PCA) Visualization — iobr_pca
text: data Input data PCA: matrix data frame. .matrix Logical indicating input matrix. Default TRUE. scale Logical indicating whether scale data. Default TRUE. .log Logical indicating whether log-transform data. Default FALSE. pdata Data frame sample IDs grouping information. id_pdata Column name `pdata` sample IDs. Default "ID". group Column name `pdata` grouping variable. Default NULL. geom.ind Type geometric representation points. Default "point". cols Color scheme groups. Default "normal". palette Color palette groups. Default "jama". repel Logical indicating whether repel overlapping points. Default FALSE. ncp Number principal components retain. Default 5. axes Principal components plot (e.g., c(1, 2)). Default c(1, 2). addEllipses Logical indicating whether add concentration ellipses. Default TRUE.

title: Principal Component Analysis (PCA) Visualization — iobr_pca
text: ggplot object PCA plot.

title: Principal Component Analysis (PCA) Visualization — iobr_pca
text: Dongqiang Zeng

title: Principal Component Analysis (PCA) Visualization — iobr_pca
code: if (requireNamespace("FactoMineR", quietly = TRUE) &&   requireNamespace("factoextra", quietly = TRUE)) {   set.seed(123)   eset <- matrix(rnorm(1000), nrow = 100, ncol = 10)   rownames(eset) <- paste0("Gene", 1:100)   colnames(eset) <- paste0("Sample", 1:10)   pdata <- data.frame(     ID = colnames(eset),     group = rep(c("A", "B"), each = 5)   )   iobr_pca(eset, pdata = pdata, id_pdata = "ID", group = "group", addEllipses = FALSE) } #>  #> A B  #> 5 5  #> >>== colors for group:  #> >>== #374E55FF>>== #DF8F44FF #> Ignoring unknown labels: #> • fill : "group" #> • linetype : "group"

what: Feature Selection for Predictive or Prognostic Models Using LASSO Regression — lasso_select
title: Feature Selection for Predictive or Prognostic Models Using LASSO Regression — lasso_select
text: Applies LASSO (Least Absolute Shrinkage Selection Operator) regression construct predictive prognostic models. Supports binary survival response variables, utilizing cross-validation optimal model selection.

title: Feature Selection for Predictive or Prognostic Models Using LASSO Regression — lasso_select
code: lasso_select(   x,   y,   type = c("binary", "survival"),   nfold = 10,   lambda = c("lambda.min", "lambda.1se") )

title: Feature Selection for Predictive or Prognostic Models Using LASSO Regression — lasso_select
text: x numeric matrix. Features (e.g., gene symbols CGI) row names samples column names. y response variable vector. Can binary (0/1) survival data (e.g., survival time event status). type Character. Model type: "binary" binary response "survival" survival analysis. Default "binary". nfold Integer. Number folds cross-validation. Default 10. lambda Character. Regularization parameter selection: "lambda.min" (minimum mean cross-validated error) "lambda.1se" (one standard error minimum). Default "lambda.min".

title: Feature Selection for Predictive or Prognostic Models Using LASSO Regression — lasso_select
text: Character vector selected feature names non-zero coefficients optimal LASSO model.

title: Feature Selection for Predictive or Prognostic Models Using LASSO Regression — lasso_select
text: Dongqiang Zeng

title: Feature Selection for Predictive or Prognostic Models Using LASSO Regression — lasso_select
code: set.seed(123) gene_expression <- matrix(rnorm(100 * 20), nrow = 100, ncol = 20) rownames(gene_expression) <- paste0("Gene", 1:100) colnames(gene_expression) <- paste0("Sample", 1:20)  # Binary response example binary_outcome <- sample(c(0, 1), 20, replace = TRUE) lasso_select(   x = gene_expression,   y = binary_outcome,   type = "binary",   nfold = 5 ) #> Warning: one multinomial or binomial class has fewer than 8  observations; dangerous ground #> Warning: one multinomial or binomial class has fewer than 8  observations; dangerous ground #> Warning: one multinomial or binomial class has fewer than 8  observations; dangerous ground #> Warning: one multinomial or binomial class has fewer than 8  observations; dangerous ground #> Warning: one multinomial or binomial class has fewer than 8  observations; dangerous ground #> character(0)

what: Differential Expression Analysis Using Limma — limma.dif
title: Differential Expression Analysis Using Limma — limma.dif
text: Performs differential expression analysis using limma package given gene expression dataset. Constructs design matrix phenotype data, fits linear model, applies contrasts, computes statistics differential expression.

title: Differential Expression Analysis Using Limma — limma.dif
code: limma.dif(exprdata, pdata, contrastfml)

title: Differential Expression Analysis Using Limma — limma.dif
text: exprdata matrix rownames features like gene symbols cgi, colnames samples. pdata two-column dataframe first column matches colnames exprdata second column contains grouping variable. contrastfml character vector contrasts tested (see ?makeContrasts details).

title: Differential Expression Analysis Using Limma — limma.dif
text: Returns dataframe limma::topTable, includes genes rows columns like genelist, logFC, AveExpr, etc.

title: Differential Expression Analysis Using Limma — limma.dif
code: # Toy example with 100 genes and 6 samples set.seed(123) exprdata <- matrix(   rnorm(100 * 6),   nrow = 100,   ncol = 6,   dimnames = list(     paste0("gene", 1:100),     paste0("sample", 1:6)   ) )  # Phenotype data: 3 vs 3 pdata <- data.frame(   sample = colnames(exprdata),   group = rep(c("group1", "group2"), each = 3),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE )  # Differential expression: group1 vs group2 res <- limma.dif(   exprdata = exprdata,   pdata = pdata,   contrastfml = "group1 - group2" ) head(res) #>            logFC     AveExpr         t    P.Value adj.P.Val         B #> gene16  2.146063  0.18155652  2.637222 0.01296770 0.6279722 -4.554407 #> gene91  1.979646 -0.19771191  2.533719 0.01657904 0.6279722 -4.557543 #> gene97  1.791447  0.65590789  2.412055 0.02199743 0.6279722 -4.561178 #> gene71 -1.738285  0.91534298 -2.330224 0.02650410 0.6279722 -4.563586 #> gene88  1.551421 -0.05545564  2.105013 0.04352443 0.6279722 -4.570024 #> gene56  1.644500 -0.43974674  2.038109 0.05018179 0.6279722 -4.571876

what: Construct Mutation Matrices from MAF Data — make_mut_matrix
title: Construct Mutation Matrices from MAF Data — make_mut_matrix
text: Builds mutation presence/absence matrices MAF input (file path MAF object). Supports multiple categories: mutations, SNPs, indels, frameshift mutations. category = "multi", returns list matrices category. Compatible TCGA-formatted data.

title: Construct Mutation Matrices from MAF Data — make_mut_matrix
code: make_mut_matrix(   maf = NULL,   mut_data = NULL,   isTCGA = TRUE,   category = c("multi", "all", "snp", "indel", "frameshift"),   Tumor_Sample_Barcode = "Tumor_Sample_Barcode",   Hugo_Symbol = "Hugo_Symbol",   Variant_Classification = "Variant_Classification",   Variant_Type = "Variant_Type" )

title: Construct Mutation Matrices from MAF Data — make_mut_matrix
text: maf Character MAF object. Path MAF file already loaded MAF object. mut_data Data frame NULL. Preloaded MAF-like data (used 'maf' NULL). isTCGA Logical. Whether MAF follows TCGA conventions. Default TRUE. category Character. Mutation category: "", "snp", "indel", "frameshift", "multi". Default "multi". Tumor_Sample_Barcode Character. Column name tumor sample IDs. Default "Tumor_Sample_Barcode". Hugo_Symbol Character. Column name gene symbols. Default "Hugo_Symbol". Variant_Classification Character. Column name variant classification (e.g., Frame_Shift_Del). Default "Variant_Classification". Variant_Type Character. Column name variant type (e.g., SNP, INS, DEL). Default "Variant_Type".

title: Construct Mutation Matrices from MAF Data — make_mut_matrix

title: Construct Mutation Matrices from MAF Data — make_mut_matrix

Please ensure MAF merged data frame uses consistent column types (e.g. convert allele columns character input).

what: Merge Data Frames with Duplicated Column Names — merge_duplicate
title: Merge Data Frames with Duplicated Column Names — merge_duplicate

title: Merge Data Frames with Duplicated Column Names — merge_duplicate

title: Merge Data Frames with Duplicated Column Names — merge_duplicate
text: x Data frame. First data frame merge. y Data frame. Second data frame merge. .x Character. Column name(s) `x` used merging. .y Character. Column name(s) `y` used merging. .x Logical. Include rows `x` output. Default `FALSE`. .y Logical. Include rows `y` output. Default `FALSE`. Logical `NULL`. `NULL`, include rows `x` `y`, overriding `.x` `.y`. choose Character. data frame's duplicated non-joining columns retain: `"x"` `"y"`. Default `"x"`.

title: Merge Data Frames with Duplicated Column Names — merge_duplicate
text: Data frame resulting merging `x` `y` according specified   parameters.

title: Merge Data Frames with Duplicated Column Names — merge_duplicate
code: df1 <- data.frame(ID = 1:3, Name = c("A", "B", "C"), Value = 1:3) df2 <- data.frame(ID = 1:3, Name = c("X", "Y", "Z"), Score = 4:6)  # Merge keeping duplicated columns from x merged_df <- merge_duplicate(df1, df2,   by.x = "ID", by.y = "ID",   all.x = TRUE, choose = "x" ) #> ℹ Removing 1 duplicate column from `y` print(merged_df) #>   ID Name Value Score #> 1  1    A     1     4 #> 2  2    B     2     5 #> 3  3    C     3     6  # Merge keeping duplicated columns from y merged_df2 <- merge_duplicate(df1, df2,   by.x = "ID", by.y = "ID",   all = TRUE, choose = "y" ) #> ℹ Removing 1 duplicate column from `x`

what: Merging the duplicates from the input matrix. — merge_duplicates
title: Merging the duplicates from the input matrix. — merge_duplicates
text: case duplicate rownames input matrix (mat), function return similar matrix unique rownames duplicate cases merged together based median values. warn true warning message written case duplicates, warning message include in_type string indicate current type input matrix.

title: Merging the duplicates from the input matrix. — merge_duplicates
code: merge_duplicates(mat, warn = TRUE, in_type = NULL)

title: Merging the duplicates from the input matrix. — merge_duplicates
text: mat matrix warn warning in_type default null

what: Merge Expression Sets by Row Names — merge_eset
title: Merge Expression Sets by Row Names — merge_eset
text: Merges two three expression sets (matrices data frames) row names (gene symbols), removing duplicates. function ensures common genes across input expression sets retained.

title: Merge Expression Sets by Row Names — merge_eset
code: merge_eset(eset1, eset2, eset3 = NULL)

title: Merge Expression Sets by Row Names — merge_eset
text: eset1 First expression set (matrix data frame row names). eset2 Second expression set (matrix data frame row names). eset3 Optional third expression set. Default `NULL`.

title: Merge Expression Sets by Row Names — merge_eset
text: Merged expression set (data frame) duplicates removed.   Row names correspond gene symbols.

title: Merge Expression Sets by Row Names — merge_eset
text: Dongqiang Zeng

title: Merge Expression Sets by Row Names — merge_eset
code: # Load example data eset_stad <- load_data("eset_stad") #> ℹ Loading cached data: "eset_stad"  # Create mock expression sets with common genes common_genes <- c("TP53", "BRCA1", "EGFR", "MYC") eset1 <- matrix(rnorm(12),   nrow = 4,   dimnames = list(common_genes, paste0("S", 1:3)) ) eset2 <- matrix(rnorm(16),   nrow = 4,   dimnames = list(common_genes, paste0("S", 4:7)) )  # Merge two expression sets merged_eset <- merge_eset(eset1, eset2) #> ℹ Common genes between eset1 and eset2: 4 #> ℹ No duplicate gene symbols found. #> ✔ Final merged expression set: 4 genes x 7 samples print(dim(merged_eset)) #> [1] 4 7

title: Calculate Microenvironment Scores — microenvironmentScores
code: microenvironmentScores(adjustedScores)

text: adjustedScores Adjusted xCell scores matrix.

title: Calculate Microenvironment Scores — microenvironmentScores

what: Convert Mouse Gene Symbols to Human Gene Symbols — mouse2human_eset
title: Convert Mouse Gene Symbols to Human Gene Symbols — mouse2human_eset
text: Converts mouse gene symbols human gene symbols expression dataset. Supports using either online resource (Ensembl) local dataset conversion.

title: Convert Mouse Gene Symbols to Human Gene Symbols — mouse2human_eset
code: mouse2human_eset(   eset,   source = c("local", "ensembl"),   is_matrix = TRUE,   column_of_symbol = NULL,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Convert Mouse Gene Symbols to Human Gene Symbols — mouse2human_eset
text: eset Matrix data frame. Expression matrix genes rows. source Character. Data source conversion: "ensembl" (online) "local". Default "ensembl". Ensembl fails, use "local" uses internal `mus_human_gene_symbol` dataset. is_matrix Logical. Whether `eset` matrix gene symbols row names. Default `TRUE`. `FALSE`, `column_of_symbol` must specified. column_of_symbol Character `NULL`. Column name containing gene symbols `eset` matrix. Default `NULL`. verbose Logical. `TRUE`, prints available Ensembl datasets. Default `FALSE`.

title: Convert Mouse Gene Symbols to Human Gene Symbols — mouse2human_eset
text: Expression set human gene symbols.

title: Convert Mouse Gene Symbols to Human Gene Symbols — mouse2human_eset
text: Dongqiang Zeng

title: Convert Mouse Gene Symbols to Human Gene Symbols — mouse2human_eset
code: # Create example mouse expression data anno_gc_vm32 <- load_data("anno_gc_vm32") #> ℹ Trying mirror 1/12: <https://github.com> #> ✔ Download complete: "anno_gc_vm32" num_rows <- 200 num_cols <- 10 sample_names <- paste0("Sample", 1:num_cols) data <- matrix(runif(num_rows * num_cols), nrow = num_rows, ncol = num_cols) rownames(data) <- anno_gc_vm32$symbol[1:200] colnames(data) <- sample_names  # Convert using local database human_data <- mouse2human_eset(data, source = "local", is_matrix = TRUE) #> ℹ Trying mirror 1/12: <https://github.com> #> ✔ Download complete: "mus_human_gene_symbol" #> ℹ Row number of original eset: 200 #> ✔ 94% of probes in expression set were annotated #> ℹ Found 1 duplicate symbol, using "mean" method #> ℹ Row number after filtering duplicated gene symbol: 217

title: outputGCT — outputGCT
text: function converts gene expression dataset GCT format file.

title: outputGCT — outputGCT
code: outputGCT(input.f, output.f)

title: outputGCT — outputGCT
text: input.f data frame character string specifying path input file. character string, file tab-separated table row names. output.f character string specifying path output file.

title: outputGCT — outputGCT
text: return value. function writes dataset specified output file GCT format.

title: outputGCT — outputGCT
code: # Create a sample input data frame sample_data <- data.frame(   Gene = c("BRCA1", "TP53", "EGFR"),   Sample1 = c(10, 15, 8),   Sample2 = c(12, 18, 7),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) rownames(sample_data) <- sample_data$Gene sample_data <- sample_data[, -1]  # Convert the input data frame to GCT format and save to temporary file output_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".gct") outputGCT(sample_data, output_file)

what: Parallel Permutation Test for CIBERSORT — parallel_doperm
title: Parallel Permutation Test for CIBERSORT — parallel_doperm
text: Parallel version doPerm. Performs permutation-based sampling runs CoreAlg function iteratively using multiple CPU cores accelerate computation. function generates empirical null distribution correlation coefficients p-value calculation CIBERSORT analysis.

title: Parallel Permutation Test for CIBERSORT — parallel_doperm
code: parallel_doperm(   perm1,   X1,   Y1,   absolute1,   abs_method1,   num_cores1 = 2,   seed = NULL )

title: Parallel Permutation Test for CIBERSORT — parallel_doperm
text: perm1 Integer. Number permutations perform (\(\ge\)100 recommended). X1 Matrix data frame. Signature matrix (cell type GEP barcode). Y1 Matrix. Mixture file containing gene expression profiles. absolute1 Logical. Whether run absolute mode (default: FALSE). abs_method1 String. Method absolute mode: 'sig.score' '.sumto1'. num_cores1 Integer. Number CPU cores parallel computation (default: 2). seed Integer. Random seed reproducibility. NULL (default), uses current random state. Set specific value (e.g., 123) reproducible results across runs.

title: Parallel Permutation Test for CIBERSORT — parallel_doperm
text: List containing: dist Numeric vector correlation coefficients permutations,     representing empirical null distribution.

title: Parallel Permutation Test for CIBERSORT — parallel_doperm
text: function utilizes foreach doParallel packages distribute permutation iterations across multiple cores. automatically handles cluster setup/teardown via .exit() prevent resource leaks. Note: Windows users may experience slower performance due socket-based parallelization (PSOCK) versus forking Unix systems.

title: Parallel Permutation Test for CIBERSORT — parallel_doperm
code: # \donttest{ X <- matrix(rnorm(1000), nrow = 100) Y <- matrix(rnorm(500), nrow = 100) rownames(X) <- rownames(Y) <- paste0("Gene", 1:100)  result <- parallel_doperm(   perm1 = 100, X1 = X, Y1 = Y,   absolute1 = FALSE, abs_method1 = "sig.score", num_cores1 = 2 ) #> Warning: already exporting variable(s): perm_indices, Y1, X1, absolute1, abs_method1 str(result$dist) #>  num [1:100, 1] 0.129 0.132 0.254 0.159 0.125 ... #>  - attr(*, "dimnames")=List of 2 #>   ..$ : chr [1:100] "result.1" "result.2" "result.3" "result.4" ... #>   ..$ : NULL # }

title: Toy STAD Phenotype Data — pdata_stad
text: data frame containing clinical pathological annotations TCGA stomach adenocarcinoma (STAD) cohort. row corresponds one tumour sample can matched columns eset_stad via ID column. dataset typically used together eset_stad examples survival analysis, subgroup comparison immune deconvolution IOBR package.

title: Toy STAD Phenotype Data — pdata_stad
code: data(pdata_stad)

title: Toy STAD Phenotype Data — pdata_stad
text: data frame one row per TCGA-STAD sample 8 variables: ID Character. TCGA sample barcode, matching column names   eset_stad. stage Factor. Pathological stage (e.g. "Stage_I",   "Stage_II", "Stage_III", "Stage_IV"). status Factor. Vital status last follow-  ("Alive" "Dead"). Lauren Factor. Lauren classification gastric cancer   ("Intestinal", "Diffuse", "Mixed" NA). subtype Factor. Molecular subtype (e.g. "CIN", "EBV",   "GS", "MSI"). EBV Factor. EBV status tumour ("Positive"   "Negative"). time Numeric. Overall survival follow-time, typically   measured months. OS_status Integer/binary. Overall survival status indicator.   (1 = death, 0 = censored)

what: Removing Batch Effect from Expression Sets — remove_batcheffect
title: Removing Batch Effect from Expression Sets — remove_batcheffect
text: Removes batch effects expression datasets using sva::ComBat (microarray/TPM data) sva::ComBat_seq (RNA-seq count data). Generates PCA plots visualize data correction.

title: Removing Batch Effect from Expression Sets — remove_batcheffect
code: remove_batcheffect(   eset1,   eset2,   eset3 = NULL,   id_type,   data_type = c("array", "count", "tpm"),   cols = "normal",   palette = "jama",   log2 = TRUE,   check_eset = TRUE,   adjust_eset = TRUE,   repel = FALSE,   path = NULL )

title: Removing Batch Effect from Expression Sets — remove_batcheffect
text: eset1 First expression set (matrix data frame genes rows). eset2 Second expression set. eset3 Optional third expression set. Use `NULL` available. id_type Type gene ID expression sets (e.g., `"ensembl"`, `"symbol"`). Required count data normalization. data_type Type data: `"array"`, `"count"`, `"tpm"`. Default `"array"`. cols Color scale PCA plot. Default `"normal"`. palette Color palette PCA plot. Default `"jama"`. log2 Whether perform log2 transformation. Default `TRUE`. Ignored count data. check_eset Whether check expression sets errors. Default `TRUE`. adjust_eset Whether adjust expression sets removing problematic features. Default `TRUE`. repel Whether add repelling labels PCA plot. Default `FALSE`. path Directory results saved. Default `NULL` (display ).

title: Removing Batch Effect from Expression Sets — remove_batcheffect
text: Expression matrix batch correction.

title: Removing Batch Effect from Expression Sets — remove_batcheffect
text: Zhang Y, et al. ComBat-seq: batch effect adjustment RNA-seq count data. NAR Genomics Bioinformatics. 2020;2(3):lqaa078. doi:10.1093/nargab/lqaa078 Leek JT, et al. sva package removing batch effects unwanted variation high-throughput experiments. Bioinformatics. 2012;28(6):882-883.

title: Removing Batch Effect from Expression Sets — remove_batcheffect
text: Dongqiang Zeng

title: Removing Batch Effect from Expression Sets — remove_batcheffect
code: eset_stad <- load_data("eset_stad") #> ℹ Loading cached data: "eset_stad" eset_blca <- load_data("eset_blca") #> ℹ Trying mirror 1/12: <https://github.com> #> ✔ Download complete: "eset_blca" # \donttest{ eset_corrected <- remove_batcheffect(   eset_stad[1:1000, 1:5], eset_blca[1:1000, 1:5],   id_type = "ensembl",   data_type = "count" ) #> ℹ The two expression matrices share 995 features #> ℹ Processing method: sva::ComBat_seq #> Found 2 batches #> Using null model in ComBat-seq. #> Adjusting for 0 covariate(s) or covariate level(s) #> Estimating dispersions #> Fitting the GLM model #> Shrinkage off - using GLM estimates for parameters #> Adjusting the data #> ℹ Loading cached data: "anno_grch38" #> ℹ Using local annotation (anno_grch38) for TPM conversion #> ℹ No duplicate gene symbols found. #> ✔ Applied log2 transformation #> ℹ Count data processed with ComBat_seq #> ✔ Applied log2 transformation #>  #> eset1 eset2  #>     5     5  #> >>== colors for group:  #> >>== #374E55FF>>== #DF8F44FF #> ✔ Applied log2 transformation #>  #> eset1 eset2  #>     5     5  #> >>== colors for group:  #> >>== #374E55FF>>== #DF8F44FF #> ℹ Log2 transformation not necessary (data appears to already be log-scaled) #>  #> eset1 eset2  #>     5     5  #> >>== colors for group:  #> >>== #374E55FF>>== #DF8F44FF # }

what: Remove Duplicate Gene Symbols in Gene Expression Data — remove_duplicate_genes
title: Remove Duplicate Gene Symbols in Gene Expression Data — remove_duplicate_genes
text: function addresses duplicate gene symbols gene expression dataset selecting highest-expressing instance among duplicates. Users can choose mean, standard deviation, sum ranking criterion selection. useful preparing data duplicates can lead issues downstream analyses.

title: Remove Duplicate Gene Symbols in Gene Expression Data — remove_duplicate_genes
code: remove_duplicate_genes(eset, column_of_symbol, method = c("mean", "sd", "sum"))

title: Remove Duplicate Gene Symbols in Gene Expression Data — remove_duplicate_genes
text: eset data frame matrix representing gene expression data, gene symbols one columns. column_of_symbol name column containing gene symbols `eset`. method ranking method use selecting among duplicate gene symbols: `"mean"` mean expression, `"sd"` standard deviation, `"sum"` sum expression values. Default `"mean"`.

title: Remove Duplicate Gene Symbols in Gene Expression Data — remove_duplicate_genes
text: modified version `eset` duplicate gene symbols   reduced single entry (highest-ranking one). gene symbols   set row names returned data frame.

title: Remove Duplicate Gene Symbols in Gene Expression Data — remove_duplicate_genes
text: Important: function performs selection, aggregation. duplicate genes, retains highest-ranking instance (based specified method) discards others.

title: Remove Duplicate Gene Symbols in Gene Expression Data — remove_duplicate_genes
text: Dongqiang Zeng

title: Remove Duplicate Gene Symbols in Gene Expression Data — remove_duplicate_genes
code: # Load and annotate expression data eset_stad <- load_data("eset_stad") #> ℹ Loading cached data: "eset_stad" anno_rnaseq <- load_data("anno_rnaseq") #> ℹ Loading cached data: "anno_rnaseq" eset_stad <- anno_eset(eset = eset_stad, annotation = anno_rnaseq) #> ℹ Row number of original eset: 60483 #> ✔ 100% of probes in expression set were annotated #> ℹ Found 2098 duplicate symbols, using "mean" method #> ℹ Row number after filtering duplicated gene symbol: 50139 eset_stad <- tibble::rownames_to_column(as.data.frame(eset_stad), var = "id")  # Create duplicate gene names for demonstration eset_stad[2:3, "id"] <- "MT-CO1"  # Check duplicates before sum(duplicated(eset_stad$id)) #> [1] 2  # Remove duplicates using mean expression as ranking criterion eset_stad <- remove_duplicate_genes(   eset = eset_stad,   column_of_symbol = "id",   method = "mean" ) #> ℹ Found 2 duplicate symbols. Using "mean" for ranking. #> ✔ Reduced to 50137 unique genes  # Check duplicates after sum(duplicated(rownames(eset_stad))) #> [1] 0

what: Calculate Signature Score Using PCA, Mean, or Z-score Methods — sigScore
title: Calculate Signature Score Using PCA, Mean, or Z-score Methods — sigScore
text: Computes signature scores gene expression data using Principal Component Analysis (PCA), mean-based, z-score approaches.

title: Calculate Signature Score Using PCA, Mean, or Z-score Methods — sigScore
code: sigScore(eset, methods = c("PCA", "mean", "zscore"))

title: Calculate Signature Score Using PCA, Mean, or Z-score Methods — sigScore
text: eset Normalized expression matrix genes (signature) rows samples columns. methods Scoring method: `"PCA"` (default) principal component 1, `"mean"` mean expression, `"zscore"` z-score normalized mean.

title: Calculate Signature Score Using PCA, Mean, or Z-score Methods — sigScore
text: Numeric vector length `ncol(eset)`; score summarizing rows   `eset`.

title: Calculate Signature Score Using PCA, Mean, or Z-score Methods — sigScore
text: Dorothee Nickles, Dongqiang Zeng

title: Calculate Signature Score Using PCA, Mean, or Z-score Methods — sigScore
code: # Load example data eset_stad <- load_data("eset_stad") #> ℹ Loading cached data: "eset_stad" eset <- count2tpm(eset_stad, idType = "ensembl") #> ℹ Loading cached data: "anno_grch38" #> ℹ Using local annotation (anno_grch38) for TPM conversion #> ! Omitting 3985 genes without length information #> ℹ Found 1679 duplicate symbols. Using "mean" for ranking. #> ✔ Reduced to 54658 unique genes  # Get signature genes signature_tme <- load_data("signature_tme") genes <- signature_tme[["CD_8_T_effector"]] genes <- genes[genes %in% rownames(eset)]  # Calculate scores (only if enough genes are available) if (length(genes) >= 2) {   score_pca <- sigScore(eset = eset[genes, ], methods = "PCA")   score_mean <- sigScore(eset = eset[genes, ], methods = "mean")   score_zscore <- sigScore(eset = eset[genes, ], methods = "zscore") }

what: Signature Box Plot with Statistical Comparisons — sig_box
title: Signature Box Plot with Statistical Comparisons — sig_box
text: Creates box plots visualize signature distributions across groups optional statistical pairwise comparisons. Supports data frames Seurat objects single-cell data visualization.

title: Signature Box Plot with Statistical Comparisons — sig_box
code: sig_box(   data,   signature,   variable,   palette = "nrc",   cols = NULL,   jitter = FALSE,   point_size = 5,   angle_x_text = 0,   hjust = 0.5,   show_pairwise_p = TRUE,   show_overall_p = FALSE,   return_stat_res = FALSE,   size_of_pvalue = 6,   size_of_font = 10,   assay = NULL,   slot = "scale.data",   scale = FALSE )

title: Signature Box Plot with Statistical Comparisons — sig_box
text: data Data frame Seurat object containing signature grouping variable. signature Character string specifying column name (feature name Seurat) signature values plot y-axis. variable Character string specifying grouping variable column name x-axis. palette Character string specifying color palette name. Default `"nrc"`. cols Character vector custom fill colors. `NULL`, palette used. Default `NULL`. jitter Logical indicating whether add jittered points box plot. Default `FALSE`. point_size Numeric value specifying size jittered points. Default 5. angle_x_text Numeric value specifying rotation angle x-axis labels (degrees). Default 0. hjust Numeric value specifying horizontal justification x-axis labels. Default 0.5. show_pairwise_p Logical indicating whether display pairwise comparison p-values groups. Default `TRUE`. show_overall_p Logical indicating whether display overall group difference p-value. Default `FALSE`. return_stat_res Logical indicating whether return statistical test results instead plot. Default `FALSE`. size_of_pvalue Numeric value specifying font size p-values. Default 6. size_of_font Numeric value specifying base font size. Default 10. assay Character string specifying assay name (Seurat objects). Default `NULL`. slot Character string specifying slot name (Seurat objects). Default `"scale.data"`. scale Logical indicating whether scale signature values (z-score transformation). Default `FALSE`.

title: Signature Box Plot with Statistical Comparisons — sig_box
text: `return_stat_res = FALSE`, returns ggplot2 object.   `return_stat_res = TRUE`, returns data frame containing statistical test   results.

title: Signature Box Plot with Statistical Comparisons — sig_box
text: Dongqiang Zeng

title: Signature Box Plot with Statistical Comparisons — sig_box
code: tcga_stad_pdata <- load_data("tcga_stad_pdata") sig_box(   data = tcga_stad_pdata,   signature = "TMEscore_plus",   variable = "subtype",   jitter = TRUE,   palette = "jco" ) #> Warning: The `size` argument of `element_line()` is deprecated as of ggplot2 3.4.0. #> ℹ Please use the `linewidth` argument instead. #> ℹ The deprecated feature was likely used in the IOBR package. #>   Please report the issue at <https://github.com/IOBR/IOBR/issues>.

title: Batch Signature Box Plots for Group Comparisons — sig_box_batch
text: input Data frame Seurat object containing data analysis. vars Character vector. Features variables analyze. `pattern_vars = TRUE`, treated regular expression patterns. groups Character vector. Grouping variable(s) comparison. pattern_vars Logical indicating whether treat `vars` regular expression patterns matching column names. Default `FALSE`. path Character string `NULL`. Directory save plots. `NULL`, plots saved. Default `NULL`. index Integer. Starting index plot filenames. Default `0`. angle_x_text Numeric. Angle x-axis labels degrees. Default `0`. hjust Numeric. Horizontal justification x-axis labels. Default `0.5`. palette Character. Color palette plots. Default `"jama"`. cols Character vector `NULL`. Custom colors plot elements. jitter Logical indicating whether add jittered points. Default `FALSE`. point_size Numeric. Size points. Default `5`. size_of_font Numeric. Base font size. Default `8`. size_of_pvalue Numeric. Size p-value text. Default `4.5`. show_pvalue Logical indicating whether display p-values. Default `TRUE`. return_stat_res Logical indicating whether return statistical results instead saving plots. Default `FALSE`. assay Character string `NULL`. Assay type Seurat objects. slot Character. Data slot Seurat objects. Default `"scale.data"`. scale Logical indicating whether scale data analysis. Default `FALSE`. height Numeric. Height plots inches. Default `5`. width Numeric. Width plots inches. Default `3.5`. fig_type Character. File format plots (e.g., `"pdf"`, `"png"`). Default `"pdf"`. max_count_feas Integer. Maximum number features analyze `pattern_vars = TRUE`. matched variables exceed limit, first `max_count_feas` features used. Default `30`.

title: Perform Gene Set Enrichment Analysis (GSEA) — sig_gsea
text: deg Data frame containing differential expression results gene symbols log fold changes. genesets List custom gene sets enrichment analysis. `NULL`, MSigDB gene sets used based `org` `category`. Default `NULL`. path Character string specifying directory path saving results. Default `NULL`. gene_symbol Character string specifying column name `deg` containing gene symbols. Default `"symbol"`. logfc Character string specifying column name `deg` containing log fold change values. Default `"log2FoldChange"`. org Character string specifying organism. Options `"hsa"` (Homo sapiens) `"mus"` (Mus musculus). Default `"hsa"`. msigdb Logical indicating whether use MSigDB gene sets. Default `TRUE`. category Character string specifying MSigDB category (e.g., `"H"` Hallmark, `"C2"` curated gene sets). Default `"H"`. subcategory Character string specifying MSigDB subcategory filter gene sets. Default `NULL`. palette_bar Character string integer specifying color palette bar plots. Default `"jama"`. palette_gsea Integer specifying color palette GSEA plots. Default `2`. cols_gsea Character vector specifying custom colors GSEA enrichment plots. `NULL`, colors automatically generated. Default `NULL`. cols_bar Character vector specifying custom colors enrichment bar plot. `NULL`, colors automatically generated. Default `NULL`. show_bar Integer specifying number top enriched gene sets display bar plot. Default `10`. show_col Logical indicating whether display color names bar plot. Default `FALSE`. show_plot Logical indicating whether display GSEA enrichment plots. Default `FALSE`. show_gsea Integer specifying number top significant gene sets generate GSEA plots. Default `8`. show_path_n Integer specifying number pathways display GSEA plots. Default `20`. plot_single_sig Logical indicating whether generate separate plots significant gene set. Default `TRUE`. project Character string specifying project name output files. Default `"custom_sig"`. minGSSize Integer specifying minimum gene set size analysis. Default `10`. maxGSSize Integer specifying maximum gene set size analysis. Default `500`. verbose Logical indicating whether display progress messages. Default `TRUE`. seed Logical indicating whether set random seed reproducibility. Default `FALSE`. fig.type Character string specifying file format saving plots (e.g., `"pdf"`, `"png"`). Default `"pdf"`. print_bar Logical indicating whether save print bar plot. Default `TRUE`.

title: Perform Gene Set Enrichment Analysis (GSEA) — sig_gsea
text: List containing: Data frame -regulated enriched gene sets Data frame -regulated enriched gene sets Complete GSEA results plot_top GSEA enrichment plot top gene sets

title: Perform Gene Set Enrichment Analysis (GSEA) — sig_gsea
text: Dongqiang Zeng

title: Perform Gene Set Enrichment Analysis (GSEA) — sig_gsea
code: set.seed(123) genes <- c(   "TP53", "BRCA1", "EGFR", "MYC", "KRAS", "PTEN", "APC", "RB1",   "CDKN2A", "VHL", "ATM", "ATR", "CHEK2", "PALB2", "RAD51", "MDM2",   "CDK4", "CDK6", "CCND1", "CCNE1", "CDK2", "E2F1", "E2F2", "E2F3",   "ARF1", "ARF3", "ARF4", "ARF5", "ARF6", "GSK3B", "AKT1", "AKT2",   "PIK3CA", "PIK3CB", "PIK3CD", "PIK3CG", "PIK3R1", "PIK3R2", "PIK3R3" ) deg <- data.frame(   symbol = genes,   log2FoldChange = rnorm(length(genes), mean = 0, sd = 2),   padj = runif(length(genes), 0, 0.1) ) signature <- list(   DNA_Repair = c(     "TP53", "BRCA1", "ATM",     "ATR", "CHEK2", "PALB2", "RAD51"   ),   Cell_Cycle = c(     "TP53", "MYC",     "RB1", "CDKN2A", "CDK4",     "CDK6", "CCND1", "CCNE1",     "CDK2", "E2F1", "E2F2", "E2F3"   ),   PI3K_AKT = c(     "AKT1", "AKT2",     "PIK3CA", "PIK3CB", "PIK3CD",     "PIK3CG", "PIK3R1", "PIK3R2", "PIK3R3"   ) ) # \donttest{ res <- sig_gsea(   deg = deg,   genesets = signature,   path = tempdir(),   show_plot = FALSE,   print_bar = FALSE ) #> ℹ Mapping gene symbols to Entrez IDs for "hsa" #>  #> 'select()' returned 1:1 mapping between keys and columns #> Warning: number of columns of result is not a multiple of vector length (arg 1) #> ✔ Signature data saved to /tmp/Rtmpl7xRpT/file1e362ad7780.csv #> 'select()' returned 1:1 mapping between keys and columns #> Warning: 3.57% of input gene IDs are fail to map... #> ℹ Running GSEA analysis... #> ✔ GSEA results written to: /tmp/Rtmpl7xRpT/1-H_GSEA_significant_results.csv #> ℹ Most significant gene sets: Cell_Cycle print(names(res)) #> [1] "up"       "down"     "all"      "plot_top" # }

what: Signature Heatmap with Optional Annotations — sig_heatmap
title: Signature Heatmap with Optional Annotations — sig_heatmap
text: Generates heatmap selected features grouped categorical variable, optional conditional (annotation) bars. Supports palette customization, scaling, size controls, output saving.

title: Signature Heatmap with Optional Annotations — sig_heatmap
code: sig_heatmap(   input,   id = "ID",   features,   group,   condition = NULL,   id_condition = "vars",   col_condition = "condition",   cols_condition = NULL,   scale = FALSE,   palette = 2,   cols_heatmap = NULL,   palette_group = "jama",   show_col = FALSE,   show_palettes = FALSE,   cols_group = NULL,   show_plot = TRUE,   width = 8,   height = NULL,   size_col = 10,   size_row = 8,   angle_col = 90,   column_title = NULL,   row_title = NULL,   show_heatmap_col_name = FALSE,   path = NULL,   index = NULL )

title: Signature Heatmap with Optional Annotations — sig_heatmap
text: input Data frame containing ID, grouping variable, feature columns. id Character string. Column name sample identifier. Default `"ID"`. features Character vector. Feature (column) names include heatmap. group Character string. Grouping variable column name. condition Data frame `NULL`. Optional annotation table variable-condition mapping. Default `NULL`. id_condition Character string. Column name `condition` feature IDs. Default `"vars"`. col_condition Character string. Column name `condition` condition labels. Default `"condition"`. cols_condition Character vector. Colors conditions. scale Logical indicating whether scale values row. Default `FALSE`. palette Integer character. Palette index/name heatmap colors. Default `2`. cols_heatmap Character vector. Custom colors heatmap gradient. palette_group Character string. Palette name group colors. Default `"jama"`. show_col Logical indicating whether display color vector. Default `FALSE`. show_palettes Logical indicating whether print palette options. Default `FALSE`. cols_group Character vector. Custom colors groups. show_plot Logical indicating whether print heatmap. Default `TRUE`. width Numeric. Plot width inches. Default `8`. height Numeric `NULL`. Plot height inches. Auto-calculated `NULL`. size_col Numeric. Font size column labels. Default `10`. size_row Numeric. Font size row labels. Default `8`. angle_col Numeric. Rotation angle column labels degrees. Default `90`. column_title Character string `NULL`. Title column annotation. row_title Character string `NULL`. Title row annotation. show_heatmap_col_name Logical indicating whether show column names. Default `FALSE`. path Character string `NULL`. Output directory saving heatmap. index Integer `NULL`. Index appended filename. Default `NULL`.

title: Signature Heatmap with Optional Annotations — sig_heatmap
text: tidyHeatmap object. Saves PDF `path` provided.

title: Signature Heatmap with Optional Annotations — sig_heatmap
text: Dongqiang Zeng

title: Signature Heatmap with Optional Annotations — sig_heatmap
code: # \donttest{ tcga_stad_sig <- load_data("tcga_stad_sig") #> ℹ Trying mirror 1/12: <https://github.com> #> ✔ Download complete: "tcga_stad_sig" tcga_stad_pdata <- load_data("tcga_stad_pdata") input <- merge(tcga_stad_pdata, tcga_stad_sig, by = "ID") feas <- grep("MCPcounter", colnames(input), value = TRUE) sig_heatmap(input = input, features = feas, group = "subtype", scale = TRUE) #> ℹ Creating heatmap with 10 features # }

what: Generate Heatmap for Signature Data — sig_pheatmap
title: Generate Heatmap for Signature Data — sig_pheatmap
text: Creates heatmap signature data grouping variables, offering flexible options colors, clustering, output formats using ComplexHeatmap.

title: Generate Heatmap for Signature Data — sig_pheatmap
code: sig_pheatmap(   input,   feas,   group,   group2 = NULL,   group3 = NULL,   ID = "ID",   path = NULL,   cols1 = "random",   cols2 = "random",   cols3 = "random",   seed = 54321,   show_col = FALSE,   palette1 = 1,   palette2 = 2,   palette3 = 3,   cluster_cols = TRUE,   palette_for_heatmape = 6,   scale.matrix = TRUE,   cellwidth = 1,   cellheight = 9,   show_colnames = FALSE,   fig.type = "pdf",   width = 6,   height = NULL,   file_name_prefix = 1 )

title: Generate Heatmap for Signature Data — sig_pheatmap
text: input Data frame variables columns. feas Character vector. Feature names (columns) include heatmap. group Character string. Column name primary grouping variable. group2 Character string `NULL`. Optional secondary grouping variable. group3 Character string `NULL`. Optional tertiary grouping variable. ID Character string. Column name sample identifiers. Default `"ID"`. path Character string `NULL`. Directory save output files. `NULL`, heatmap saved. Default `NULL`. cols1 Character vector `"random"` `"normal"`. Colors primary group. Default `"random"`. cols2 Character vector `"random"` `"normal"`. Colors secondary group. Default `"random"`. cols3 Character vector `"random"` `"normal"`. Colors tertiary group. Default `"random"`. seed Integer. Random seed color generation. Default `54321`. show_col Logical indicating whether display colors. Default `FALSE`. palette1 Integer. Palette primary group. Default `1`. palette2 Integer. Palette secondary group. Default `2`. palette3 Integer. Palette tertiary group. Default `3`. cluster_cols Logical indicating whether cluster columns. Default `TRUE`. palette_for_heatmape Integer. Palette number heatmap. Default `6`. scale.matrix Logical indicating whether scale matrix. Default `TRUE`. cellwidth Numeric. Width cell points. Default `1`. cellheight Numeric. Height cell points. Default `9`. show_colnames Logical indicating whether show column names. Default `FALSE`. fig.type Character string. File format saving. Default `"pdf"`. width Numeric. Width saved figure inches. Default `6`. height Numeric `NULL`. Height saved figure inches. Calculated `NULL`. file_name_prefix Character numeric. Prefix saved file name. Default `1`.

title: Generate Heatmap for Signature Data — sig_pheatmap
text: list containing: p_anno Annotation data frame p_cols List cluster colors plot ComplexHeatmap object eset Transformed expression matrix

title: Generate Heatmap for Signature Data — sig_pheatmap
text: Dongqiang Zeng

title: Generate Heatmap for Signature Data — sig_pheatmap
code: tcga_stad_sig <- load_data("tcga_stad_sig") #> ℹ Loading cached data: "tcga_stad_sig" tcga_stad_pdata <- load_data("tcga_stad_pdata") input <- merge(tcga_stad_pdata, tcga_stad_sig, by = "ID") feas <- grep("MCPcounter", colnames(input), value = TRUE) sig_pheatmap(   input = input, feas = feas, group = "subtype",   scale.matrix = TRUE, path = tempdir() ) #> ℹ Heatmap palettes: 1 (pheatmap), 2 (peach), 3 (blues), 4 (virids), 5 (reds), 6 (RdBu), 7 (navy_firebrick), 8 (magma) #> ℹ Random palettes: 1 (palette1), 2 (palette2), 3 (palette3), 4 (palette4) #> ℹ Using random seed: 54321 #> ✔ Heatmap saved to: /tmp/Rtmpl7xRpT/1-pheatmap-subtype.pdf

what: Plot ROC Curves and Compare Them — sig_roc
title: Plot ROC Curves and Compare Them — sig_roc
text: Generates Receiver Operating Characteristic (ROC) curves multiple predictors optionally performs statistical comparisons .

title: Plot ROC Curves and Compare Them — sig_roc
code: sig_roc(   data,   response,   variables,   fig.path = NULL,   main = NULL,   file.name = NULL,   palette = "jama",   cols = NULL,   alpha = 1,   compare = FALSE,   smooth = TRUE,   compare_method = "bootstrap",   boot.n = 100 )

title: Plot ROC Curves and Compare Them — sig_roc
text: data Data frame containing predictor variables binary outcome. response Character. Name binary outcome variable `data`. variables Character vector. Names predictor variables ROC curves. fig.path Character `NULL`. Directory path save output PDF. Default `NULL`. main Character `NULL`. Main title ROC plot. Default `NULL`. file.name Character `NULL`. Output PDF filename without extension. Default `"0-ROC multiple variables"`. palette Character. Color palette ROC curves. Default `"jama"`. cols Character vector `NULL`. Custom colors ROC curves. Default `NULL`. alpha Numeric. Transparency level (1 = opaque, 0 = transparent). Default `1`. compare Logical. Whether perform statistical comparison AUCs. Default `FALSE`. smooth Logical. Whether smooth ROC curves. Default `TRUE`. compare_method Character. Method comparing ROC curves. Default `"bootstrap"`. boot.n Integer. Number bootstrap replications. Default `100`.

title: Plot ROC Curves and Compare Them — sig_roc
text: list containing: auc.Data frame AUC values confidence intervals legend.name Vector legend entries plot p.`compare = TRUE`, data frame p-values comparisons

title: Plot ROC Curves and Compare Them — sig_roc
text: Dongqiang Zeng

title: Plot ROC Curves and Compare Them — sig_roc
code: # \donttest{ tcga_stad_pdata <- load_data("tcga_stad_pdata") sig_roc(   data = tcga_stad_pdata, response = "OS_status",   variables = c("TMEscore_plus", "GZMB", "GNLY") ) #> ℹ Input data preview: #> Registered S3 method overwritten by 'pROC': #>   method   from             #>   plot.roc spatstat.explore #> Setting levels: control = 0, case = 1 #> Setting direction: controls > cases #> Setting levels: control = 0, case = 1 #> Setting direction: controls < cases #> Setting levels: control = 0, case = 1 #> Setting direction: controls > cases  #> $auc.out #>                   Name   AUC      AUC CI #> auc.ci   TMEscore_plus 0.564 0.504-0.624 #> auc.ci.1          GZMB 0.493 0.431-0.555 #> auc.ci.2          GNLY 0.507 0.449-0.568 #>  #> $legend.name #> [1] "TMEscore_plus  AUC =  0.564" "GZMB  AUC =  0.493"          #> [3] "GNLY  AUC =  0.507"          #>  # }

what: Generate Kaplan-Meier Survival Plot for Signature — sig_surv_plot
title: Generate Kaplan-Meier Survival Plot for Signature — sig_surv_plot
text: Creates Kaplan-Meier survival plots given signature gene, automatic cutoff determination. Generates three types plots: optimal cutoff (best cutoff), tertile-based (3 groups), median split (2 groups).

title: Generate Kaplan-Meier Survival Plot for Signature — sig_surv_plot
code: sig_surv_plot(   input_pdata,   signature,   project = "KM",   ID = "ID",   time = "time",   status = "status",   time_type = "month",   break_month = "auto",   cols = NULL,   palette = "jama",   show_col = TRUE,   mini_sig = "score",   fig.type = "png",   save_path = NULL,   index = 1 )

title: Generate Kaplan-Meier Survival Plot for Signature — sig_surv_plot
text: input_pdata Data frame survival data signature scores. signature Character string. Column name target signature. project Character string. Project name output. Default `"KM"`. ID Character string. Column name sample IDs. Default `"ID"`. time Character string. Column name survival time. Default `"time"`. status Character string. Column name survival status. Default `"status"`. time_type Character string. Time unit (`"month"` `"day"`). Default `"month"`. break_month Numeric `"auto"`. Time axis breaks. Default `"auto"`. cols Character vector. Optional custom colors. palette Character string. Color palette `cols` provided. Default `"jama"`. show_col Logical indicating whether show colors. Default `TRUE`. mini_sig Character string. Label low score group. Default `"score"`. fig.type Character string. File format. Default `"png"`. save_path Character string `NULL`. Directory saving plots. `NULL`, plots saved. Default `NULL`. index Integer. Index multiple plots. Default `1`.

title: Generate Kaplan-Meier Survival Plot for Signature — sig_surv_plot
text: list containing: data Processed input data group assignments plots Combined survival plots
