what: Subgroup Survival Analysis Using Cox Proportional Hazards Models — subgroup_survival
title: Subgroup Survival Analysis Using Cox Proportional Hazards Models — subgroup_survival
text: Extracts hazard ratios (HR) 95 proportional hazards models across specified subgroups.

title: Subgroup Survival Analysis Using Cox Proportional Hazards Models — subgroup_survival
code: subgroup_survival(   pdata,   time_name = "time",   status_name = "status",   variables,   object )

title: Subgroup Survival Analysis Using Cox Proportional Hazards Models — subgroup_survival
text: pdata Data frame containing variables, follow-time, outcome. time_name Character. Column name follow-time. Default `"time"`. status_name Character. Column name event status (1/0). Default `"status"`. variables Character vector. Subgrouping variables (processed independently). object Character. Variable interest used Cox model. levels "High" "Low", recode "High" 0 "Low" 1 calling.

title: Subgroup Survival Analysis Using Cox Proportional Hazards Models — subgroup_survival
text: Data frame summarizing subgroup Cox results (HR, CI, p-value).

title: Subgroup Survival Analysis Using Cox Proportional Hazards Models — subgroup_survival
text: Dongqiang Zeng

title: Subgroup Survival Analysis Using Cox Proportional Hazards Models — subgroup_survival
code: subgroup_data <- load_data("subgroup_data") input <- subset(subgroup_data, time > 0 & !is.na(status) & !is.na(AJCC_stage))  # Binary variable example res_bin <- subgroup_survival(   pdata = input, time_name = "time", status_name = "status",   variables = c("ProjectID", "AJCC_stage"), object = "score_binary" )  # Continuous variable example res_cont <- subgroup_survival(   pdata = input, time_name = "time", status_name = "status",   variables = c("ProjectID", "AJCC_stage"), object = "score" )

what: Generate Kaplan-Meier Survival Plots for Categorical Groups — surv_group
title: Generate Kaplan-Meier Survival Plots for Categorical Groups — surv_group
text: Creates Kaplan-Meier survival plots data grouped categorical variable. Handles binary multi-level categorical groups customizable plot aesthetics.

title: Generate Kaplan-Meier Survival Plots for Categorical Groups — surv_group
code: surv_group(   input_pdata,   target_group,   ID = "ID",   levels = c("High", "Low"),   reference_group = NULL,   project = NULL,   time = "time",   status = "status",   time_type = "month",   break_month = "auto",   cols = NULL,   palette = "jama",   mini_sig = "score",   save_path = NULL,   fig.type = "pdf",   index = 1,   width = 6,   height = 6.5,   font.size.table = 3 )

title: Generate Kaplan-Meier Survival Plots for Categorical Groups — surv_group
text: input_pdata Data frame containing survival data grouping variables. target_group Name column containing grouping variable. ID Name column unique identifiers. Default "ID". levels Names levels target_group (binary groups). Default c("High", "Low"). reference_group Reference level binary comparison. Default NULL. project Optional title plot. Default NULL. time Name column follow-times. Default "time". status Name column event indicators. Default "status". time_type Units: "month" "day". Default "month". break_month X-axis break interval. "auto", calculated automatically. Default "auto". cols Color vector plot lines. Default NULL. palette Color palette name. Default "jama". mini_sig Prefix label variables. Default "score". save_path Directory saving plot. Default NULL. fig.type File format: "pdf" "png". Default "pdf". index Identifier file naming. Default 1. width Plot width. Default 6. height Plot height. Default 6.5. font.size.table Font size risk table. Default 3.

title: Preprocess TCGA RNA-seq Data — tcga_rna_preps
text: Preprocesses TCGA RNA-seq data modifying sample types, transforming data, annotating genes based specified parameters.

title: Preprocess TCGA RNA-seq Data — tcga_rna_preps
code: tcga_rna_preps(   eset,   id_type = c("ensembl", "symbol"),   input_type = c("log2count", "count"),   output = c("tumor", "tumor_normal"),   output_type = c("tpm", "log2tpm", "count"),   annotation = TRUE )

title: Preprocess TCGA RNA-seq Data — tcga_rna_preps
text: eset Matrix data frame. RNA-seq gene expression matrix TCGA. id_type Character. Gene identifier type: "ensembl" "symbol". Default "ensembl". input_type Character. Input data type: "log2count" "count". Default "log2count". output Character. Sample type: "tumor" "tumor_normal". Default "tumor". output_type Character. Output data type: "tpm", "log2tpm", "count". Default "tpm". annotation Logical. Whether perform gene annotation. Default TRUE.

title: Preprocess TCGA RNA-seq Data — tcga_rna_preps
text: Preprocessed gene expression matrix.

title: Preprocess TCGA RNA-seq Data — tcga_rna_preps
text: Dongqiang Zeng

code: eset_stad <- load_data("eset_stad") #> ℹ Loading cached data: "eset_stad" eset <- tcga_rna_preps(   eset = eset_stad, id_type = "ensembl", input_type = "count",   output = "tumor", output_type = "tpm", annotation = TRUE ) #> ℹ Sample type distribution: #>  #>     #> 10  #> ℹ Adjacent normal samples removed #> ℹ TCGA barcode reduced to 12 digits #> ℹ Duplicate barcode check: #>    Mode   FALSE  #> logical      10  #> ℹ Converting count to TPM #> ℹ Loading cached data: "anno_grch38" #> ℹ Using local annotation (anno_grch38) for TPM conversion #> ! Omitting 3985 genes without length information #> ℹ Found 1679 duplicate symbols. Using "mean" for ranking. #> ✔ Reduced to 54658 unique genes

title: TCGA-STAD Clinical and Molecular Annotation Data — tcga_stad_pdata

EBV – EBV infection status TMEscore_plus – continuous tumor-micro-environment score TMEscore_plus_binary – High/Low TME classification time – follow-time (months) OS_status – 0 = alive, 1 = dead ARID1A, PIK3CA – driver-gene mutation status MALAT1 – lncRNA expression remaining columns – gene-expression values additional   clinical/molecular annotations

title: Identification of TME Cluster — tme_cluster
text: Performs TME (Tumor Microenvironment) clustering analysis using various clustering methods. Supports feature selection, scaling, automatic determination optimal cluster number.

title: Identification of TME Cluster — tme_cluster
code: tme_cluster(   input,   features = NULL,   pattern = NULL,   id = NULL,   scale = TRUE,   method = "kmeans",   min_nc = 2,   max.nc = 6 )

title: Identification of TME Cluster — tme_cluster
text: input Data frame containing input dataset. features Vector features use clustering. Default NULL (uses columns pattern-selected columns). pattern Regular expression pattern selecting features. Default NULL. id Column name identifiers. Default NULL (uses row names). scale Logical indicating whether scale features. Default TRUE. method Clustering method. Default "kmeans". min_nc Minimum number clusters evaluate. Default 2. max.nc Maximum number clusters evaluate. Default 6.

title: Identification of TME Cluster — tme_cluster
text: Data frame cluster assignments appended.

title: Identification of TME Cluster — tme_cluster
text: Dongqiang Zeng

title: Identification of TME Cluster — tme_cluster
code: set.seed(123) input_data <- data.frame(   ID = paste0("Sample", 1:20),   xCell_Tcells = rnorm(20),   xCell_Bcells = rnorm(20),   xCell_Macrophages = rnorm(20),   Other_feature = rnorm(20) ) result <- tme_cluster(   input = input_data,   pattern = "xCell",   id = "ID",   method = "kmeans" ) #> ℹ Best number of TME clusters: 3 #> ℹ Cluster distribution: #>  1  2  3  #> 10  8  2  table(result$cluster) #>  #> TME1 TME2 TME3  #>   10    8    2

title: TME Deconvolution Methods — tme_deconvolution_methods
text: methods currently supported : `mcpcounter`: MCP-counter immune stromal cell populations `epic`: EPIC immune, stromal, cancer cell fractions `xcell`: xCell 64 immune stromal cell types `cibersort`: CIBERSORT 22 immune cell types `cibersort_abs`: CIBERSORT absolute mode `ips`: Immunophenoscore calculation `estimate`: ESTIMATE stromal/immune/estimate scores `svr`: Support Vector Regression (custom reference) `lsei`: Least Squares Equality/Inequality constraints `timer`: TIMER cancer-specific immune estimation `quantiseq`: quanTIseq RNA-seq immune deconvolution

title: Transform NA, Inf, or Zero Values in Data — transform_data
code: data_matrix <- data.frame(   A = c(1, 2, NA, 4, Inf),   B = c(Inf, 2, 3, 4, 5),   C = c(0, 0, 0, 1, 2) )  # Replace NAs with 0 transform_data(data_matrix, feature = c("A", "B"), data_type = "NA") #>     A   B C #> 1   1 Inf 0 #> 2   2   2 0 #> 3   0   3 0 #> 4   4   4 1 #> 5 Inf   5 2  # Replace Inf values with the mean of the column transform_data(data_matrix,   feature = c("A", "B"),   data_type = "Inf", into = "mean" ) #>          A   B C #> 1 1.000000 3.5 0 #> 2 2.000000 2.0 0 #> 3       NA 3.0 0 #> 4 4.000000 4.0 1 #> 5 2.333333 5.0 2  # Replace zeros with -1 in column C transform_data(data_matrix, feature = "C", data_type = "zero", into = -1) #>     A   B  C #> 1   1 Inf -1 #> 2   2   2 -1 #> 3  NA   3 -1 #> 4   4   4  1 #> 5 Inf   5  2

title: The xCell Analysis Pipeline — xCellAnalysis
text: Returns xCell cell types enrichment scores tumor microenvironment deconvolution. Uses ssGSEA-based enrichment analysis spillover compensation estimate cell type proportions gene expression data.

title: The xCell Analysis Pipeline — xCellAnalysis
code: xCellAnalysis(   expr,   signatures = NULL,   genes = NULL,   spill = NULL,   rnaseq = TRUE,   file.name = NULL,   scale = TRUE,   alpha = 0.5,   save.raw = FALSE,   cell.types.use = NULL )

title: The xCell Analysis Pipeline — xCellAnalysis
text: expr Gene expression data matrix row names gene symbols columns samples. signatures GMT object signatures. `NULL`, uses xCell defaults. genes Character vector genes use analysis. `NULL`, uses xCell defaults. spill Spillover object adjusting scores. `NULL`, uses xCell defaults. rnaseq Logical indicating whether use RNA-seq (TRUE) array (FALSE) spillover parameters. Default `TRUE`. file.name Character string saving scores. Default `NULL`. scale Logical indicating whether use scaling. Default `TRUE`. alpha Numeric value override spillover alpha parameter. Default `0.5`. save.raw Logical indicating whether save raw scores. Default `FALSE`. cell.types.use Character vector cell types use. `NULL`, uses available cell types. Default `NULL`.

text: matrix adjusted xCell scores.

title: IOBR 2.2.0
text: Fixed functions wrote user’s home filespace (working directory) default. Writing now occurs explicit path / save_path / output.dir provided. Affected functions: eset_distribution(), find_outlier_samples(), iobr_cor_plot(), sig_pheatmap(), sig_box_batch(), plotPurity(), IPS_calculation(), find_mutations(), sig_gsea(), get_cor(), batch_sig_surv_plot(), format_signatures(), creat_folder(). Examples updated use tempdir() file writing demonstrated.

title: IOBR 2.2.0
text: find_mutations(): Fixed semantic naming error file_name variable used store directory paths. Renamed output_dir clarity. Fixed ggsave() parameter order issues. iobr_cor_plot(): Fixed ggsave() parameter order issues. correct order filename first, plot. surv_group(): Fixed ggsave() parameter order issues. roc_time(): Fixed ggsave() parameter order issues. batch_sig_surv_plot(): Changed default save_path file.path(tempdir(), "Multiple-KM-plot") NULL prevent automatic directory creation. format_signatures(): Changed parameter name output_name output_path consistency. Added validation requiring output_path save_signature = TRUE. find_outlier_samples(): Added validation requiring project save = TRUE. plotPurity(): Changed default output.dir "estimated_purity_plots" NULL.

title: IOBR 2.2.0
text: CRAN Size Compliance: Moved large datasets (>5MB total) R/sysdata.rda data/ GitHub Releases meet CRAN package size requirements. Data now downloaded -demand cached locally. Added load_data() function unified data access (supports sysdata, exported data, GitHub-hosted datasets) Added download_iobr_data() multiple mirror support (GitHub, ghproxy.vip, gh-proxy.org, ghfast.top) Added add_iobr_mirror() custom mirror configuration Added list_github_datasets() show available remote datasets Added clear_iobr_cache() manage downloaded data Automatic Caching: Downloaded datasets cached user’s cache directory persist across package updates. Fixed count2tpm() handle symbol-based gene IDs robustly.

title: IOBR 2.2.0
text: following datasets now hosted GitHub downloaded first use: - Reference matrices: BRef, TRef, lm22 - Annotations: anno_gc_vm32, anno_grch38, anno_hug133plus2, anno_illumina, anno_rnaseq - Example datasets: tcga_stad_sig, imvigor210_sig, eset_stad, sig_stad, eset_gse62254, etc. - Gene sets: hallmark, kegg, go_bp, go_cc, go_mf, reactome, msig_immune, msig_sc - Cell markers: cancer_type_genes, cellmarkers, common_genes, immuneCuratedData, ips_gene_set, SI_geneset, mRNA_cell_default, mus_human_gene_symbol, onco_sig, PurityDataAffy - Reference data: xCell.data, quantiseq_data - Signatures: signature_collection_citation, signature_metabolism, signature_sc, signature_tumor

title: IOBR 2.1.1
text: Added Docker support automated image builds publishing GitHub Container Registry (ghcr.io/iobr/iobr). image based rocker/tidyverse includes IOBR dependencies pre-installed (#Docker). Added Conda/Mamba installation instructions README easier environment setup. Expanded iobr_cor_plot() support multi-group (3+) comparisons using Kruskal-Wallis test, addition existing two-group Wilcoxon test (#28).

title: IOBR 2.1.1
text: Fixed generateRef_DEseq2() handle sparse single-cell RNA-seq data using type = "poscounts" size factor estimation, preventing errors genes contain least one zero. Fixed batch_kruskal() output format correctly display mean-centered values groups. Fixed count2tpm() computation results align previous correct versions. Updated count2tpm() documentation clarify gene identifiers converted gene symbols output regardless input ID type. Wrapped long-running examples \dontrun{} \donttest{} comply CRAN check time limits.

title: IOBR 2.1.1
text: Refactored internal helper functions count2tpm.R better code organization maintainability. Updated load_data() documentation include additional available datasets.
